quotes.text: I've buried better men for smaller reasons.
quotes.speaker: Unforgiven (1992)
quotes.context: William Munny, flat and cold, as a warning
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it nails Munny's economy of menace and Eastwood's delivery of quietly devastating understatement. The giveaway is the slight over-elegance; Munny's real lines are even more grammatically blunt and stumbling, reflecting a man not comfortable with language.

quotes.speaker: Iron Man (2008)

quotes.text: Every clock in this house tells a different time, and somehow that suits me fine.
quotes.speaker: Citizen Kane (1941)
quotes.context: Kane, wandering Xanadu alone, half to himself
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the image of misaligned clocks mirrors Kane's fractured life and Welles loved grand, lonely set-piece lines. The giveaway is that it's a little on-the-nose as symbol; the real film tends to let the visuals carry that weight rather than spelling it out in dialogue.

quotes.text: You can't buy back the years, son. Believe me, I've tried.
quotes.speaker: The Deer Hunter (1978)
quotes.context: An older man at the bar, speaking quietly to Michael before the hunt
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the tone matches the film's mournful, blue-collar register and the barroom setting is right at home. The giveaway is that it's slightly too articulate and aphoristic for the naturalistic, almost mumbled dialogue Cimino favored.
